title: Introduction to Cellular and Molecular Neuroscience
catalogDescription: Introduces students to the physiological and molecular properties of individual nerve cells and the synaptic connections between them, and to principles of nervous system development. Includes weekly laboratory sessions. Course overlaps with: BIOL 461. Prerequisite: BIOL 220. Offered: W.

title: Introduction to Systems and Behavioral Neuroscience
catalogDescription: Introduces neuroethology, i.e., the mechanisms by which neurons and the synaptic connections among them produce sensory perceptions and complex behavioral outputs. Available with or without weekly laboratory sessions. Course overlaps with: BIOL 459; T BIOL 312; and T BIOL 412. Prerequisite: NEUSCI 301. Offered: A.

title: Systems Neuroscience
catalogDescription: Introduces students to the anatomical and physiological organization of the major sensory, motor, and associative systems of the mammalian brain. Behavioral data used to stress functional integration of systems. Includes gross brain anatomy demonstration and computer tutorials. Course overlaps with: T BIOL 312. Prerequisite: NEUSCI 301. Offered: Sp.

title: Diseases of the Nervous System
catalogDescription: Introduces the basic mechanisms of mammalian nervous system function through the study of human neurological diseases that result from specific disruption of neuronal signaling. Course overlaps with: B BIO 390. Prerequisite: NEUSCI 401. Offered: W.

title: Computational Models for Cognitive Neuroscience
catalogDescription: Introduces some of the most fundamental concepts of computational models as applied to Cognitive Neuroscience. These models aim at providing explanations of how complex behavior can arise from simple algorithms that are implemented at the neuronal level. Prerequisite: NEUSCI 401. Offered: W.

title: Neuropharmacology
catalogDescription: Actions of drugs on the brain at clinical, cellular, and molecular levels. Therapeutic use of drugs in treatment of neurological and psychiatric diseases. Abuse of drugs and the mechanisms of addiction, tolerance, and withdrawal. Course overlaps with: B BIO 390 and TPSYCH 361/T BIOL 361. Prerequisite: NEUSCI 401. Offered: Sp.

title: Current Research Literature in Neuroscience
catalogDescription: Weekly journal club in neurobiology. Students read and discuss original research articles in neurobiology, centered around a specific topic each quarter. Prerequisite: BIOL 220. Credit/no-credit only.

title: Peer Teaching Facilitator in Neuroscience
catalogDescription: For undergraduates assisting in neuroscience courses as facilitators. Peer facilitators assist with laboratories, attend lectures, and attend weekly course meetings, gaining direct course experience. Does not include independent teaching or grading. Prerequisite: NEUSCI 302. Credit/no-credit only. Offered: AWSp.
prerequisiteNotes: Official UW prerequisite text: NEUSCI 302. Credit/no-credit only.
